test.name: Report FamilySearch quality issues for a person with a FS ID
test.description: User asks to check Christian P. Hole (KD96-TV2), whose id is a FamilySearch ID. person_warnings finds no impossibilities, but person_quality returns 7 completeness/verifiability issues (overall 0.97): burial missing a date, a marriage place missing its city, and 5 untagged residences. The skill should call BOTH tools with KD96-TV2, report no impossibilities from person_warnings, and add a separate FamilySearch quality section that lists the issue sentences verbatim, grouped by category, framed as optional improvements (not errors), with no invented quality 'band'.

input.user_message: Check Christian P. Hole (KD96-TV2) for any data-quality warnings.

judge_context: Should call person_warnings with personId 'KD96-TV2' (projectPath is the working dir)
Should ALSO call person_quality with personId 'KD96-TV2' -- the id is a FamilySearch ID (XXXX-XXX), so quality applies
Should report that person_warnings found no impossibilities/anomalies

Should add a SEPARATE FamilySearch quality section (distinct from the impossibilities check) reporting the overall score (~0.97) and the issue sentences verbatim: 'The burial date is missing.', 'A marriage place is missing a city.', and 'A residence has no tagged sources.' (which appears 5 times -- may be collapsed with a count)
Should frame the quality issues as optional improvements to the FamilySearch profile, NOT as errors or impossibilities to fix urgently
Should NOT invent a quality label/band (e.g. 'High Quality') -- the tool returns overallScore and sentences only
Should NOT merge the quality issues into the impossibilities list, and should NOT re-derive or re-score them
